subject: Project Management
title: The FBI's Virtual Case File
description: A fast breakdown of The FBI's Virtual Case File for builders.

hook.text: After 9/11, the FBI spent $170 million on a case management system. It never worked.

buildup.text: The Virtual Case File was supposed to let agents share intelligence digitally instead of on paper.

discovery.text: Requirements changed 400 times during development. Every change rippled through the entire system.

twist.text: After four years, the FBI scrapped the entire project. $170 million — deleted.

climax.text: The replacement project, Sentinel, also ran over budget — but eventually worked after an Agile reboot.

punchline.text: Four hundred requirement changes. Zero working software.

quiz.question: Why did the FBI's Virtual Case File project fail?
options: The FBI ran out of agents to use it
